programmes.show_description: A stressed 76-year-old is rushed to St George's with a deep head cut. And a mother remembers learning to love the colour of her skin after suffering racial abuse growing up.
The Goggleboxers watch Eurovision, Faking It, Virgin Island, The Four Seasons, Night Coppers, Inside Our Minds, and Naked and Afraid.
Gamer Lauren is looking for her perfect player number two to help her reboot her love life. And Ryan is searching for a silver fox with a dad bod.
...Experiment: Chelsea and George seek a threesome, but will it help Chelsea move past George's sexually adventurous past and infidelity? Jess and Ben host a naked dinner party.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Brilliant Builds: This episode celebrates one of Kirstie's biggest builds as we look back at how Keeley and Terence completely overhauled their Woodford home beyond recognition.

programmes.show_name: George Clarke's Old House New Home
programmes.show_description: An Edwardian home in Manchester is dragged kicking and screaming into the modern day. And in London, Flo's 1930s flat needs a rejig to create the sociable space she craves.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: As a blanket of snow falls, the Bakers ensure that all the animals are fed and protected and make safe an old ash tree that's perilously close to the farmhouse.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In the Pyrenees, Becky and Gav have a mountain to climb to complete their holiday lets. And will Mark and Gary's gîte be awarded a five-star rating?
...the Sun: With their summer deadline looming and so many delays to the build, Jasmine and Jon draft in an architect. And Jon makes a splash as he attempts to fix the swimming pool. (Ep6)
Martin and Kate are taking the big step of buying their first property together overseas, near Spain's beautiful Mar Menor. Jean Johansson shows them five options for their £90,000 budget.
Loretta and Carl want the perfect Spanish bolthole in Mojácar, where they can relax and unwind whenever they like. Laura Hamilton shows them what their £100k can buy.
Donna and Paul race to finish a risky new floor, six metres up. Terry and Ashley gamble on their most lavish suite yet. And can Sharon and Tim complete their tower suite?

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Brilliant Builds: Kirstie and Phil look back at Kathleen and Tom's three-bed Victorian semi in Newcastle, where chaos reigned and discipline was desperately needed.
Welsh valleys. Dog lover Kerry keeps her food simple and her welcome extravagant, with a Welsh choir awaiting her guests in her driveway. But a canine criticism leaves a bad taste. (Ep1/5)
Welsh valleys. Foodie Dave hopes to impress with home-grown herbs, hand-picked fruit and technical expertise, on a night featuring toilet inspections and rollercoaster revelations. (Ep2/5)
Welsh valleys. Shop owner Tasnim Sardar, who claims to be an amazing cook, plans an authentic Pakistani party. But Kerry has a bone to pick with her host. (Ep3/5)
Welsh valleys. Ryan puts a lot of thought into his dinner party, but each of his courses ends in disaster. Can dancing and drinks in the hot tub distract his guests? (Ep4/5)
Welsh valleys. On the last night in Wales, financial advisor Gill Leonard welcomes her guests with a fire-breathing dragon, and makes a remarkable revelation... (Ep5/5)
Puppies Poppy and Fizz struggle to adapt to kennel life and may need to be separated if they hope to be re-homed. And is Moose the Staffy more dog than most families can handle?
Keith Brymer Jones and Marj Hogarth crack on with the next stage of their 163-year-old chapel renovation, and throw a gigantic vintage clothes sale to raise much-needed funds.
Bill goes deeper into ancient local tales, riding a junk boat through the breathtakingly beautiful limestone karsts of Ha Long Bay, where legends of dragons live on. (Ep6)
From polar bear raves to AI sea shanties and the collapse of the teaspoon industry, Bill leads a magical, musical mystery tour of the human mind in his 2025 stand-up at Edinburgh Playhouse.
Bill becomes the first British comedian to tread the boards of the Royal Opera House as he goes down the rabbit hole with Google reviews of Poundland, and Samuel Pepys in the modern age.

programmes.show_name: George Clarke's Old House New Home
programmes.show_description: Masum and Maria's south London Victorian terraced home no longer works for their family. Louis and Sarah tackle the upstairs of their 16th-century gatehouse in Hove.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: Matt begins work on his dream woodland hut and wildlife hideaway, while Nicola, Luke and Molly make the pond area more accessible for Mike and Janice.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Portugal, Richard and Simon's bar relaunches after closing for a week. In Italy, a drought causes problems at Lisa and Paul's holiday lets. Plus: mattress mayhem in France.
...the Sun: As work progresses, the endless dust, noise and stress take their toll. And after Jon has another setback, the family begin to wonder if the build is really worth it... (Ep7)
Tracy from Southport is searching for a country home with B&B potential around the lakes of Languedoc in France. Can Jonnie Irwin find her perfect property on a budget of £100,000?
Anne-Marie and Adrian consider Los Cristianos in southern Tenerife their second home... so why not buy one there? Can Jean Johansson find the perfect place for £160k?
Terry and Ashley race to finish the 401 Suite and rehang a 200-crystal chandelier. Belinda and Lee tackle tower mould. Can Vivienne and Simon finish a bathroom mural in time?

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Brilliant Builds: Kirstie and Phil look back at Catherine and Nigel's dated, 70s-style bungalow in Larne. Was the addition of a garage a love-it game changer?
Eastbourne. Accident-prone trainee teacher Kirsty Davenport hosts with a simple menu and some creative entertainment. But have the group already tired of Russell's constant jokes? (Ep1/5)
Eastbourne. The guests have high expectations for Ann-Marie Hamilton's menu. And she plans to reveal her fun side with a surprise singalong in the garden. (Ep2/5)
Eastbourne. Hairdresser Becci Moloney's outdoor bar is a hit with her female guests. But Russell and Adam don't know what to make of her collection of recycled toilets... (Ep3/5)
Eastbourne. Self-confessed foodie Adam has been talking his night up all week. But his guests are surprised by a menu that doesn't amount to much more than posh bangers and mash. (Ep4/5)
Eastbourne. Funnyman Russell Turnbull hosts the final night. He's played his culinary cards close to his chest, but can he actually cook? (Ep5/5)
